description: A V8 sandbox bypass vulnerability exists due to incorrect code entrypoint tag handling in the JS dispatch table. The root cause is that `kJSEntrypointTag` is aliased to `kDefaultCodeEntrypointTag` (both equal 0) in `src/sandbox/code-entrypoint-tag.h`. As a result, `JSDispatchTable::IsCompatibleCode` in `src/sandbox/js-dispatch-table-inl.h` only checks `code->entrypoint_tag() != kJSEntrypointTag`, which inadvertently allows builtins using `kDefaultCodeEntrypointTag` (namely `CCallDescriptor` and `CEntryDummyDescriptor`) to pass the compatibility check and be installed onto JS dispatch table entries. Builtins such as `CEntry_Return1_ArgvOnStack_NoBuiltinExit`, `MemCopyUint8Uint8`, and `MemMove` can therefore be dispatched via JS call sites. An attacker with sandbox write primitives can install these builtins on a dispatch entry and invoke them from JavaScript: `CEntry_Return1_ArgvOnStack_NoBuiltinExit` performs an indirect call to the rbx register (attacker-controlled), enabling full PC control, while `MemCopyUint8Uint8`/`MemMove` enable arbitrary memory writes. This completely bypasses the V8 sandbox.

fixes.evidence_text: [sandbox] Ensure only JS linkage builtins are added to dispatch tables
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://crrev.com/c/6955106 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
